_doc: LOCAL-ONLY backlog for the in-place Ralph loop (gitignored; the shell owns task status — the agent must NOT edit this file). Schema: docs/HARNESS.md §8. All tasks are verifiable with NO live paid-API calls.

tasks.title: Validation gates: declare contracts on real jobs so gates fire
tasks.do: T006 built the gate MECHANISM but no real job declares contracts, so zero gates fire. Declare `produces`/`consumes` ArtifactContracts on the perfumes + places pipeline stages (e.g. fetch produces raw pages, parse consumes them; ingest produces CSV rows, resolver consumes them) so the executor enforces a gate on each DAG edge and fails LOUD on format drift. Verify against cached data/ fixtures — NO live calls.
tasks.doneWhen: Perfumes + places stages declare produces/consumes contracts; gates derive and fire between stages (pipeline start-log gate count > 0); a unit test proves a satisfied contract passes and a drifted one fails the consumer; no live API/scrape used in verification.

tasks.title: Validation gates: surface on the DAG graph + failure logs
tasks.do: Represent validation gates on the pipeline DAG graph: show a gate marker on the producer→consumer edge in a pipeline run, make a failed gate visually distinct, and make the gate-failure run/logs reachable from the graph. Expose gate info via the API so the dashboard can render it.
tasks.doneWhen: Gates appear on the pipeline-run DAG between producer→consumer; a failed gate is visually distinct and links to its failure logs; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Per-item progress: emit ctx.progress(i/N) + per-item logs across all jobs
tasks.do: Jobs that process N items don't report progress as they go — e.g. perfumes-find-url works 41 items but never logs `1/41 …` and the run % doesn't advance. Make every item-processing job emit `ctx.progress(i/N*100)` and a per-item log line as it works (sensible denominators), not just a final summary. Apply across ALL such jobs, not just find-url.
tasks.doneWhen: Item-processing jobs emit per-item progress + a per-item log line; the run % advances during the run (verified against cached fixtures / scratch DB, no live calls); covers every item-loop job.

tasks.title: Move the Chrome profile to a shared framework location
tasks.do: The persistent Chrome profile lives inside the perfumes job (`src/jobs/perfumes/data/chrome-profile`). Now that `src/core/browser.ts` is the shared helper, move it to a framework-level, configurable path so it isn't perfumes-specific and other scrapers (e.g. the places resolver) can reuse the warmed, trusted profile. Keep it gitignored.
tasks.doneWhen: The Chrome profile path is framework-level + configurable (not under src/jobs/perfumes/data); the new location is gitignored; perfumes fetch still uses it; typecheck + tests green.

tasks.title: Trim the README — it's a wall of text
tasks.do: The README is too verbose. Trim it: drop or shrink the `## Layout` file-tree, and present the worked-example pipelines compactly/scannably instead of long prose. Keep the essential 'what it is / how to run it'.
tasks.doneWhen: README is noticeably shorter + scannable; the Layout file-tree is gone or minimal; example pipelines are compact; the core run/install instructions remain accurate.

tasks.title: Don't count cancelled runs as failed
tasks.do: The overview 'Failed' tile (and other status rollups) lump `cancelled` in with failed/partial. A cancelled run is a deliberate stop, not a failure. Give `cancelled` its own treatment — not counted as failed — on the overview tiles and any other status aggregation.
tasks.doneWhen: Cancelled runs are no longer counted as failed on the overview (and any other rollup); dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Dismissed vs ignored: park ignored items, show only on overview
tasks.do: Manually-ignored items currently reappear as 'stuck' and inflate stuck counts on the pipelines tab + pipeline/job detail. Fix the root cause (ignore must persist; the stuck query must exclude ignored; a re-run must not resurrect an ignored item) AND split the concepts: ignored items are parked and shown ONLY on the overview via a new fifth 'Ignored' tile (which filters to them) — never counted as stuck, and not shown on the pipelines tab, pipeline detail, or job detail.
tasks.doneWhen: Ignored items never count as stuck and don't appear outside the overview; a 5th 'Ignored' overview tile shows + filters them; an ignored item stays ignored across re-runs; unit tests cover persistence + exclusion-from-stuck.

tasks.title: Services: explicit 'remove cap' control
tasks.do: On the Services edit-limits form it's unclear how to clear a cap back to 'no cap' (inputs just show placeholder text). Add an explicit control (e.g. a 'No cap' / clear button per field) that sets the value to no-cap (null) unambiguously, instead of relying on typing/blanking.
tasks.doneWhen: Each limit field has a clear UI way to set it back to no-cap (null); saving persists null; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Services: colour free services green
tasks.do: On the Services page, colour the 'free' service pill green (paid stays as-is) so free vs paid reads at a glance.
tasks.doneWhen: Free-service pills render green; paid unchanged; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Keep cron schedule expressions on one line
tasks.do: Cron schedule expressions (e.g. perfumes `0 2 * * *`) wrap onto two lines even on a wide desktop. Keep the schedule on one line where possible (e.g. white-space:nowrap on the schedule cell/mono span). Keep this in mind for the mobile styling pass too.
tasks.doneWhen: Schedule cron expressions render on a single line on desktop; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Drop standalone jobs — everything is a pipeline
tasks.do: Remove the concept of standalone (non-pipeline) jobs: model every unit of work as a pipeline (a single job = a one-stage pipeline). Drop the dashboard 'Standalone jobs' section, the job-level standalone schedule/enable path (pipelines own scheduling), and the registry/scheduler handling for non-member jobs. Decide how a lone job is declared (implicit one-job pipeline). Now that demo is gone there are no standalone jobs to migrate.
tasks.doneWhen: No standalone-job code path remains; every job belongs to a pipeline; the dashboard has no Standalone section; scheduler/registry handle only pipeline-driven jobs; typecheck + tests green; docs updated.

tasks.title: Rename 'pipeline' → 'workflow' everywhere
tasks.do: Rename the 'pipeline' concept to 'workflow' everywhere: types (PipelineDefinition→WorkflowDefinition), DB tables (pipelines, pipeline_jobs, pipeline_runs, pipeline_run_logs → workflow*) WITH a migration that preserves existing rows, store functions, the `*.pipeline.ts` discovery convention (→ `*.workflow.ts`), pipeline-executor.ts, API routes (/api/pipelines, /pipeline-runs), dashboard routes/pages (/pipelines, /pipeline-runs) + labels, the `pipeline` field on jobs, and all docs. Mechanical but wide.
tasks.doneWhen: No 'pipeline' naming remains for the concept (code, DB, routes, UI, docs); a DB migration moves existing pipeline_* data to workflow_* without loss; typecheck + tests + dashboard build green; docs updated.

tasks.title: Programmatic input ingestion into a pipeline's data/raw
tasks.do: Add a way to push fresh input into a pipeline's data/raw without manual file copying — e.g. a daemon API endpoint (POST upload) and/or an `npm run ingest` CLI that writes into the right pipeline's data/raw — so a run can process the latest data programmatically.
tasks.doneWhen: Fresh input can be pushed into a pipeline's data/raw via an API endpoint or CLI (no manual copy); covered by a test against a scratch dir; documented.

tasks.title: Mobile dashboard styling pass
tasks.do: Make the dashboard responsive on mobile (it currently looks bad on a phone). Build an automated check that loads each dashboard page in a headless browser at an iPhone-class viewport (iPhone 17, ~402px wide) against a scratch DB, and asserts basic mobile styling is respected — no horizontal overflow, no text crossing element boundaries, no weird truncation/clipping. Then fix the responsive CSS (tables, stat cards, nav, DAG graph, schedule cells) until it passes.
tasks.doneWhen: An automated iPhone-class-viewport (iPhone 17, ~402px) check covers every dashboard page and asserts no horizontal overflow / no boundary-crossing / no weird truncation; the dashboard passes it; dashboard build green; the check is runnable + its result recorded in the worklog.

tasks.title: Decide + wire up where pipeline output goes
tasks.do: Decide where pipeline output ultimately lands (Obsidian vault / static site / cloud sync) and build the step that delivers it there, instead of leaving output in data/out. The destination is a human decision first.
tasks.doneWhen: Destination decided with the human; the delivery step is built and writes output there.

tasks.title: More intuitive remote dashboard access (alias)
tasks.do: Make remote dashboard access friendlier than typing the tailnet IP:port on mobile — a Tailscale MagicDNS hostname, a `tailscale serve` nice URL, or a phone home-screen shortcut. Partly operational (Tailscale admin / the machine), not CI-verifiable.
tasks.doneWhen: The dashboard is reachable from the phone via a friendly alias/shortcut (human confirms).

tasks.title: Joint review of the perfumes pipeline
tasks.do: Walk through how the (overnight-built) perfumes pipeline actually works, together — it hasn't been reviewed jointly. A conversation, not a build.
tasks.doneWhen: The perfumes pipeline has been walked through together and any follow-ups captured.

tasks.title: Folder-hierarchy check-in
tasks.do: Review the repo's file/folder organisation together and confirm it's laid out the way the owner wants. Subjective, no pass/fail.
tasks.doneWhen: Repo layout reviewed together and confirmed / adjustments captured.

tasks.title: Dashboard styling — make it more joyful
tasks.do: Check in on the dashboard's overall look & feel and make it more joyful. Subjective design work needing the owner's eye.
tasks.doneWhen: Look & feel reviewed with the owner and agreed changes applied.
